item.title: It begins: Google moves to limit Ad Blockers and Privacy Extensions
item.description:
"Google Chrome’s plan to limit ad blocking extensions kicks off next week" "Google Chrome will be shutting down its older, more capable extension system, Manifest V2, in favor of exclusively using the more limited Manifest V3. The deeply controversial Manifest V3 system was announced in 2019, and the full switch has been delayed a million times, but now Google says it's really going to make the transition: As previously announced, the phase-out of older Chrome extensions is starting next week." Full Article: https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2024/05/google-starts-deprecating-older-more-capable-chrome-extensions-next-week/ So what does this mean for Chrome users? It means that your adblocking, anti-tracking and otherwise privacy-focused extensions will be made less effective, possibly completely broken. Google are an Ad business so really don't want you cutting in to their margins with plugins that prevent them and their partners from serving you ads, collecting data and generally tracking your online activity. So, what are the alternatives? Unfortunately almost all the big browsers are Chromium-based now (basically built on Chrome) - including Edge and Opera - and you're pretty much limited to no frills browsers, Firefox or browsers built on Firefox. Just before anyone recommends Brave:
"Brave is very into affiliate marketing. Just in March this year, Brave was caught running eToro affiliate marketing without the legally-required disclaimers — and Brave staff were caught deleting all mention of this from the /r/brave_browser subforum on Reddit. [Github, archive] If you’re using Brave and try to go to the Binance crypto exchange, Brave hijacks the Binance link you typed in, and autofills with its own affiliate code. This was spotted by @cryptonator1337 on Twitter earlier today. Brendan Eich, the founder and CEO of Brave, assures us that putting his referrer links into URLs that users typed in, to try to get people to click through accidentally, is all completely upright and above-board. [Twitter] This ignores the legally required disclosures for affiliate links — the disclosures that Brave also ignored for the eToro links in March. In the US, the FTC has required full disclosure of affiliate marketing since 2009 — you have to put it right there on the page. Similar rules apply in the UK and the EU. (See my Amazon disclosure at the bottom-right of this post, for example" Full Article: https://davidgerard.co.uk/blockchain/2020/06/06/the-brave-web-browser-is-hijacking-links-and-inserting-affiliate-codes/ The article is from 2020, but I've personally always been suspicious of it because of the integration of Blockchain stuff which I'm violently allergic to but it's also a Chromium-based browser. So, alternatives:
I've been using FireFox for desktop and mobile since their respective releases and my only gripe had been that they like to mix up the UI which annoys me. Outside of that, the only real issues I've heard are that it doesn't support certain kinds of more niche web content (Apple Business manager, some webgl games, web services that want direct access/control to your usb devices). I recommend that you install UBlock Origin and Facebook Container, also OldRedditRedirect for Reddit users. Another nice plugin is NoScript which blocks, among other things, JavaScript content on sites. My experience is that, while it can drastically speed up a lot of sites (Junk-heavy sites like Fandom) because it prevents junk from loading, this also frequently breaks a lot of sites. But it allows you to reenable specific aspects of the site so after a bit of a learning curve, you can turn back on the bits that allow you to use the site without the rest of the junk. Then we have a few popular Firefox forks:
Librewolf: LibreWolf is a free and open-source web browser and fork of Firefox, with an emphasis on privacy and security. Desktop only.
Palemoon: While it may look like a slightly older version of Firefox, Pale Moon is much more. Pale Moon is a fork of Firefox maintained by Dutch developer M.C. Straver, also known as Moonchild. Pale Moon's goal is to “create a full-featured, optimized browser derived from the code behind the popular Firefox browser”. Desktop only.
SeaMonkey: SeaMonkey is a free and open-source Internet suite. It is the continuation of the former Mozilla Application Suite, based on the same source code, which itself grew out of Netscape Communicator and formed the base of Netscape 6 and Netscape 7. Desktop only.
And, obviously, there is good old Safari. Apple only. You might not use plugins so aren't bothered that Google are trying to break them, but with Manifest V3 Google are setting out their intent to limit your ability to protect your privacy so they can make even more money from your browsing habits and data. Don't trust them not to go further. Edit: In response to the user going through the comments and claiming I'm spreading misinformation and clickbait: There IS a version of UBlock that works under Manifest V3, but it is 'Ublock lite'. What is the difference between MV2 UBlock and MV3 UBlock lite? Well (from the article said user obviously didn't bother to read): "A big source of skepticism around Manifest V3 is limitations around "content filtering," aka the APIs ad blockers and anti-tracking extensions use to fight ad companies like Google. Google, which makes about 77 percent of its revenue from advertising, has not published a serious explanation as to why Manifest V3 limits content filtering, and it's not clear how that aligns with the goals of "improving the security, privacy, performance and trustworthiness." Like Kewisch said, the primary goal of malicious extensions is to spy on users and slurp up data, which has nothing to do with content filtering. This is all happening while Google is building an ad system directly into Chrome, and Google properties like YouTube are making aggressive moves against ad blockers." - So Google is limiting how much filtering an extension can do. And "One dramatic change is that filtering extensions won't be able to update their rulesets themselves anymore, and any filtering updates would require a new version submitted to the Chrome extension store, which includes a potentially weeks-long security review. In the cat-and-mouse game of ad blockers, you can imagine how this could let YouTube change the ad system instantly, while any counterpunches from ad blockers could be delayed by weeks. Google now says it's possible for extensions to skip the reviews process for "safe" rule set changes, but even this is limited to "static" rulesets, not more powerful "dynamic" ones." - and Google is limiting how a plugin manages it's filters. But, yeah, I'm spreading clickbait?!

item.dc:creator: doxydad1

item.title: Firefighter adopts baby dragon!
item.description:
Hi, my name’s Tim and I’m a firefighter! A little while ago I adopted a baby dragon called Bash, and now my life is adorably chaotic. My secret ambition is to train Bash to be used in the fire service, whilst Bash's secret ambition is to steal all of my gold. Little does he know, I am poor... Watch the full video here - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BggqeFrUuag
We make comics and videos about our adventures!
Having a baby dragon in the neighbourhood definitely has many drawbacks. I think this postman might be skipping my house from now on...
My hope is that one day Bash will be used in the fire service. I think he’ll be great! He’s fireproof and can navigate through fire like a duck to water. He’s small and can climb, so he could easily locate people in a burning building. There’s only one problem… he loves starting fires.
I’ve installed fire alarms in every room and I sleep with a fire extinguisher under my pillow, which I’m blaming for the neck-hump I’m developing. I’m using gold coins as treats to help train him, which certainly gains his attention. Firefighters don’t exactly make the big bucks though, and I’m pretty sure he’s now richer than me. I think he knows it too, seeing how smug he’s been looking lately.
Having a baby dragon as a pet has definitely turned my life upside down. But I wouldn't change it for the world!
Everyday brings new lessons and challenges. We want to make more videos like this, and have a lot more stories to tell. Let us know in the comments if you want to see more Tim and Bash!
The most rewarding moments of Tim and Bash are when we receive comments telling us that they were having a really tough day, but that our comic brightened their day. This makes it feel like we are doing something purposeful. I hope these videos and comics have put a smile on your face! You can watch all 6 of our live-action videos here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jxU75Ve58J0&list=PLU-NOEuFJKIP0ff90uAQEBrtNRToYwGrI If you want to check out more Tim and Bash content, you can do so here https://www.timandbash.com/
item.dc:creator: dacrookie7

item.title: More freshly stolen trans memes to kick off pride month.
item.description:
For those who may not know, Nintendo has rereleased Thousand Year Door and rewritten Vivi's dialogue to confirm she is Trans.
Lets add a couple more sins this month
This one it important to remember, I see plenty of Trans TERF's out there and it's sad.
Ok I feel I need to clarify here based on a couple comments, this isn't about hating men, and the same user who made this version also posted the opposite version at the same time. I am trans femme so I grabbed this version since it represents ME. I do not like my masuline form and it makes me Dysphoric The prospect of eventually being able to fully embrace my feminine side brings me Joy and hope. That is what this meme is about.
:3
It's been a month already... I know I know it's not a race it's a marathon but still feels like this
*snrk*
Gibbe pls.
I still get that feeling sometimes.
This one may not be for everyone, personally that is the only part im NOT dysphoric about -_-
I like to include stuff for the trans boi's too normally but the troves were a bit dry of them today and I really need an ice cream right now.
Also includes free headpats.
This concludes this round of meme pilfering. Raids will continue until head pats and snacks are aquired.
Noir Tax, He's a dapper little gentleman.

item.title: Dam straight! And also pride month is also for those of you who haven't come out yet, you are also seen here, don't feel pressured, you are part of the community be proud... happy pride month folks!
item.description: Edit: this is already getting hate, if I've offended anyone in the community with my title I apologise. I'm an ally, you have my support, I'll always have your back....I'm sorry if the title comes across wrong in any way!!
